title: Split-board hinge — a flexible case construction
sources: Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — board attachment and hinge construction
notes: The split-board technique uses an inner board and a narrower outer strip separated by a narrow gap at the hinge line. The text-block sewing cords or tapes lace between the two layers. On closing, the case flexes at the split rather than cracking the spine cloth. An intermediate refinement on the standard case binding.
